title: TLS, Authentication, Virtual Hosts, Authorization, and Secrets · Contract Lens: Amqps Identity Verification
summary: A production client uses amqp with guest credentials, grants configure/write/read wildcards across the default virtual host, logs connection URLs, disables certificate verification, and cannot rotate credentials without downtime. Planned artifact: Add and verify the amqps identity verification increment in messaging identity, transport, and least-privilege matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use TLS with trusted CA, hostname and SNI verification, minimum protocol policy, client identity where required, and tested certificate rotation.
Design saga state, commands, events, timeouts, idempotent steps, compensations, irreversibility, operator intervention, and audit evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Amqps Identity Verification · Theory Amqps Identity Verification
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 9611 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate verified TLS and vhost access while preserving separate configure, write, and read permissions; preserve one tenant fairness invariant; then add one subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
